summary: Stellar Crazy Fingers opener, jam sandwich & soaring The Other One
blurb: The second set bursts with a blazing Crazy Fingers opening that flows into an expansive Playing In The Band → Drums → Space jam sandwich, climaxing with The Other One and China Doll. Jerry’s guitar is front‑and‑center while Brent’s keyboards drive the spacey sections. Highlights also include Sugar Magnolia and a rousing encore of The Mighty Quinn.
review:
April 19, 1986 at Berkeley Community Theater delivered one of those compact, high‑energy gems that keep Deadheads coming back for more. After an unremarkable first set, the band ignited the night with Crazy Fingers kicking off a tightly knit second set. That launch rolled straight into a monster jam sandwich—Playing In The Band → Drums → Space—that kept the groove rolling and showcased Brent’s mind‑boggling keyboard flights. The Other One surged forward with Jerry on fire, his lead cutting through the mix like a laser, while the twin drum engines of Mickey and Bill built an intense poly‑rhythmic backbone that lifted the whole performance. China Doll capped the set with a soaring climax, and Sugar Magnolia closed it out before a jubilant encore of The Mighty Quinn sent the house into a frenzy. Across multiple sources the show is praised for its crisp soundboard capture, front‑center Garcia tone on audience tapes, and a psychedelic vibe that feels both tight and exploratory—making this night a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead’s 1986 peak.

For listeners seeking the definitive version, the candyman‑sourced SBD (gd86-04-19.sbd.candyman.8568.sbeok.shnf) stands out as the cleanest, most detailed recording—crisp, sparkling, and free of dropouts—perfect for hearing every nuance of that legendary jam sandwich. Whether you’re after the pristine board mix or the raw audience energy, this Berkeley show offers a rare blend of precision and wild improvisation that epitomizes the band’s late‑’80s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are strongly positive, repeatedly praising the Crazy Fingers opener, jam sandwich (Playing In The Band → Drums → Space), The Other One, and Brent's spacey keyboards. Mixed sentiment from one source is outweighed by consistent acclaim across the others, justifying a substantial positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Top‑tier soundboard with crisp, sparkling clarity; captures the entire second set without dropouts, showcasing the band's tight jam sandwich and Garcia's front‑center guitar.

key_highlights: Blazing Crazy Fingers opener that launches a tight jam sandwich.
Intense The Other One and China Doll climax with soaring energy.
Brent Mydland’s spacey keyboards drive the psychedelic sections.
Exceptional drum/space segment highlighted by both audience and board recordings.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is front‑and‑center, intense and psychedelic, especially on The Other One and throughout the jam sandwich.
band_performance.Bob: Bob adds vocal texture on The Other One, complementing Jerry’s lead.
band_performance.Brent: Brent delivers mind‑boggling, space‑filled keyboards that propel the improvisational sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey contributes a poly‑rhythmic, high‑energy drum/space segment that fuels the set’s momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Billy’s drumming locks in with Mickey, providing the driving force behind the jam sandwich and The Other One.
